name: Lemon Queen Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter and shortening at medium speed with an electric mixer for about 2 minutes or until creamy.
2. Gradually add sugar, beating for 5 to 7 minutes.
3. Combine milk and water.
4. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt; add alternately with milk mixture, beginning and ending with flour mixture. Beat at low speed just until blended after each addition. Stir in flavorings.
5. Beat egg whites at high speed until stiff peaks form; fold into batter.
6. Pour batter into 4 greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans.
7. Bake at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cool in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes; remove from pans and cool on wire racks.
9. Combine coconut and lemon juice, tossing gently.
10. Spread Lemon Filling between layers and on top of cake; spread Lemon-Butter Frosting on sides.
11. Press coconut mixture into frosting on sides of cake.
Lemon-Butter Frosting:
1. Beat butter at medium speed of an electric mixer until creamy.
2. Add 1 cup powdered sugar, beating until blended.
3. Gradually add milk, beating until blended after each addition.
4. Beat in lemon rind and vanilla.
5. Gradually beat in remaining sugar until blended; beat to spreading consistency.
Lemon Filling:
1. Combine first 3 ingredients in a heavy saucepan.
2. Gradually stir in water and lemon juice.
3. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly for 2 to 3 minutes.
4. Stir about one-fourth of hot mixture into egg yolks; stir into remaining hot mixture.
5. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute or until mixture thickens and boils.
6. Remove from heat; chill.
Variation:
For a variation on the classic lemon cake, substitute this recipe for the lemon frosting. This makes enough glaze for the four-layer cake or the two 13 x 9-inch cakes.
Semisweet Chocolate Glaze:
1. Combine semisweet chocolate chips and whipping cream in a large glass bowl.
2. Microwave at Medium-High (50% power) for 3 1/2 minutes.
3. Remove and whisk or stir until chocolate melts and mixture is blended, about 1 to 2 minutes.
4. If chocolate isn't melted, microwave again at 50% power for 30 to 60 seconds; stir briskly.
5. Whisk or stir in butter.
6. Set aside 10 to 15 minutes or until mixture has cooled to a spreading consistency.
